parentQuestion: Which of these would you NOT find in the cerebral cortex?

scaffolds.q: The cerebral cortex is the thin outer layer of the cerebrum.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The cortex is about 2-4mm thick, covering the cerebrum's surface like a rind on an orange. This thin layer is where conscious processing happens.

scaffolds.q: The cerebral cortex is made of gray matter.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Gray matter contains neuron cell bodies, dendrites, and synapses - the computation hardware. That's what the cortex is.

scaffolds.q: Neuron cell bodies are found in gray matter.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Cell bodies (somas) are the defining feature of gray matter. The cortex is packed with them - about 16 billion neurons.

scaffolds.q: Dendrites receive signals and are found in gray matter.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Dendrites are the receiving branches of neurons. They're abundant in gray matter where synaptic connections form.

scaffolds.q: Interneurons are local circuit neurons found in gray matter.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Interneurons connect neurons within a region. The cortex has many types of interneurons modulating local circuit activity.

scaffolds.q: White matter contains myelinated axons, not cell bodies.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! White matter = axon bundles covered in myelin (fatty insulation). It's transmission cables, not processing units.

scaffolds.q: Fiber tracts are bundles of axons found in white matter, not gray matter.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Fiber tracts (like the corpus callosum) are organized bundles of myelinated axons. They live beneath the cortex in the white matter.

scaffolds.q: The cortex (gray matter) contains cell bodies, dendrites, and interneurons - but NOT fiber tracts.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Gray matter processes, white matter transmits. Fiber tracts are white matter structures found BENEATH the cortex, not in it.

scaffolds.q: You would NOT find fiber tracts in the cerebral cortex because they are white matter structures.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The answer is FIBER TRACTS. The cortex has cell bodies, dendrites, and interneurons (all gray matter components). Fiber tracts are white matter - they live deep to the cortex.
